steps.title: Map the two-part structure of the text
steps.content: The text opens by stating that P. seppenradensis is the largest ammonite in the fossil record and that scientists have been perplexed by its size since 1895. That is a mystery. The rest of the passage then describes how Soto, Price, and colleagues examined 154 fossils and concluded the species may have evolved from a smaller relative and grown as larger size helped escape predators. That is an attempt to solve the mystery.

steps.title: Match the structure to the choices and eliminate wrong ones
steps.content:
• "It states a long-standing scientific assumption and then shows how recent research has overturned that assumption.": The text presents a mystery (perplexing size), not an assumption, and the researchers offer a hypothesis rather than overturn one. ✗
  • "It describes a scientific mystery and then discusses a research team's attempt to solve that mystery.": Part one = scientists perplexed by the size (mystery); part two = Soto, Price, and colleagues examine fossils and propose an explanation (attempt to solve it). ✓
  • "It compares fossils found in Montana and England and then points out which of those fossils provides more information.": Both fossil sets are listed as evidence; no comparison or ranking between them is made. ✗
  • "It notes the discovery of a new ammonite species and then remarks on the importance of that discovery.": The species was discovered in 1895 — not new — and the text discusses its size, not the importance of the discovery. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
